_meta.hardware: Snapdragon X Elite CRD (Adreno X1-85 / DirectML via D3D12)
_meta.model: facebook/convnext-tiny-224 (ALL findings from this model only)
_meta.epistemics_warning: ⚠️ DML experiments required swapping onnxruntime-directml for onnxruntime (Python package conflict). Results reflect DML EP behavior via winml's DML DLL, not the Python onnxruntime-directml package directly. Re-validate if package setup changes.

findings.title: DML FP32 is faster and more stable than QNN GPU FP32 on the same Adreno X1-85
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: DML JIT-compiles HLSL shaders at model load time — shader compilation is done once, producing stable execution. QNN GPU EP does graph partitioning at each session creation — more overhead and jitter.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Prefer DML over QNN GPU for GPU inference (faster + more stable). DML is the primary GPU EP to optimize.
findings.confidence: medium — consistent in 3 runs each; mechanism is plausible but not confirmed by profiling
findings.do_not_generalize_to: NVIDIA/Intel GPUs (QNN GPU not available there anyway)

findings.title: NHWC transformer hurts DML (same as QNN GPU)
findings.observation: DML NHWC: p50=16.5ms, p90=21.0ms (+19% p90), std=1.89 (3.6x worse than FP32 baseline).
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: D3D12 on Adreno X1-85 does not benefit from explicit NHWC layout transforms. DML handles tensor layouts internally via HLSL; adding ORT NHWC Transposes creates overhead.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do NOT apply nhwc-transformer for DML EP.
findings.confidence: medium — single run comparison; consistent direction
findings.do_not_generalize_to: NVIDIA GPUs (NHWC may help with CUDNN)

findings.title: DML FP16 gives ~1.4x speedup with NO DVFS bimodal (unlike QNN GPU FP16)
findings.observation: DML FP16 (via Python hack, not official CLI): p50=11.8ms, p90=12.8ms, std=0.66. Clean unimodal distribution.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: DML HLSL shader compilation locks in FP16 compute paths at load time — no dynamic voltage/frequency switching surprises. QNN GPU FP16 showed DVFS bimodal distribution (some runs in high-power state, some in low-power state).
findings.action_for_autoconfig: FP16 is the primary optimization lever for DML. Unblock via #867 (--precision fp16 flag).
findings.confidence: low — experiment used Python hack (not official winml CLI). Mark as SKIPPED/CLI-gap until #867 ships.
findings.cli_gap_note: ⚠️ This finding was produced via a Python workaround, not winml CLI. Cannot be reproduced with winml build today. Blocked on #867.

findings.title: winml analyze returns 0/0/0/251 (all Unknown) for DML EP — no rule data
findings.observation: winml analyze --ep dml outputs: supported=0, partial=0, unsupported=0, unknown=251.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: DML EP supports all standard ONNX ops by design (D3D12 universal op coverage). winml analyze has no DML-specific rule data file. This is a cosmetic gap — DML actually runs all ops natively.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do not use winml analyze output to prune search space for DML. Assume all ops supported.
findings.confidence: high — confirmed by DML running all 251 ops with no CPU fallback
findings.tracked_issue: not filed — cosmetic gap, low priority

findings.title: opset 21 on DML not yet validated
findings.observation: opset 21 sweep only run on QNN NPU. DML behavior with opset 21 is unknown.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: DML uses D3D12 dispatch — different from QNN EP kernel registry. opset 21 speedup on QNN NPU may not apply.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Include opset 21 in DML search sweep. No prior data — must run experiment.

opset.rationale: dml-005: unknown. Include both in sweep.
opset.dialectical_note: ⚠️ No data on DML + opset 21. Do not assume NPU behavior transfers.

skip: w8a16 — quantization rarely helps on GPU via DML
quantization.dialectical_note: ⚠️ Quantization skip is based on general DML behavior. Some models with large weights may benefit from W8A16 even on DML. Test empirically.

compile.dialectical_note: ⚠️ DML uses HLSL, not QNN binary compilation. winml compile targets QNN EPContext only. Not applicable to DML.

graph_passes.dialectical_note: ⚠️ Same as QNN GPU: NHWC hurts on Adreno. NVIDIA/Intel may differ.
